data.title: Arkham: Bybit outflow funds have sold $200 million of stETH
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to Arkham monitoring, Bybit outflow exceeded $1 billion. $1.4 billion of ETH and stETH flowed out of Bybit, and the funds have begun to be transferred to new addresses for sale. So far, $200 million of stETH has been sold.
Address: 0x47666Fab8bd0Ac7003bce3f5C3585383F09486E2

data.title: MNT Experiences Volatility With Recent Price Fluctuations
data.description: According to BlockBeats, on February 21, market data indicated that MNT briefly fell below $0.90 before recovering to $0.9572. The cryptocurrency experienced a 24-hour decline of 7.71%.

data.title: BNB Drops Below 650 USDT with a 0.03% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Feb 21, 2025, 15:44 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has dropped below 650 USDT and is now trading at 649.650024 USDT, with a narrowed 0.03% decrease in 24 hours.

data.description: On Feb 21, 2025, 15:44 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Ethereum has dropped below 2,700 USDT and is now trading at 2,681.060059 USDT, with a narrowed 1.84% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Bybit Lianchuang: Assets in a specific ETH cold wallet were transferred, and all other cold wallets are safe
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Bybit co-founder Ben Zhou posted on X platform that about 1 hour ago, the Bybit ETH multi-signature cold wallet just transferred to our hot wallet. It seems that this particular transaction is forged, and all signers saw the forged UI, which showed the correct address, and the URL came from @safe. However, the signature information was to change the smart contract logic of our ETH cold wallet. This caused the hacker to control the specific ETH cold wallet we signed and transferred all the ETH in the cold wallet to this unidentified address. Please rest assured that all other cold wallets are safe.
All withdrawals are normal.

data.title: WalletConnect Secures $10M Token Sales Across LaunchX, CoinList, Echo and Private Sale
data.description:
The fundraising on Bitget’s LaunchX platform reached its $4 million target within two hours with 40,000 investors committing over $170 million.
The post WalletConnect Secures $10M Token Sales Across LaunchX, CoinList, Echo and Private Sale appeared first on NFTgators .
source: https://www.nftgators.com/walletconnect-secures-10m-token-sales-across-launchx-coinlist-echo-and-private-sale/

data.title: ZachXBT: More than $1.46 billion of suspicious funds outflow detected on Bybit, and follow-up is in progress
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Chain detective ZachXBT posted on his personal Telegram channel that he had detected suspicious outflows of more than $1.46 billion from Bybit, and relevant information will be updated later. The relevant address is 0x47666Fab8bd0Ac7003bce3f5C3585383F09486E2. mETH & stETH are currently being exchanged for ETH on DEX.

data.title: ZachXBT: My sources confirm that the Bybit fund outflow was a security incident
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News ZachXBT posted on his personal Telegram channel that my sources confirm it's a security incident.
In addition, ZachXBT reminded relevant personnel of major exchanges: It is recommended to blacklist the following EVM addresses.
0x47666fab8bd0ac7003bce3f5c3585383f09486e2
0xa4b2fd68593b6f34e51cb9edb66e71c1b4ab449e
0x36ed3c0213565530c35115d93a80f9c04d94e4cb
0x1542368a03ad1f03d96D51B414f4738961Cf4443
Previously, the chain detective ZachXBT posted on his personal Telegram channel that he had detected more than $1.46 billion in suspicious funds outflow from Bybit, and relevant information will be updated later. The relevant address is 0x47666Fab8bd0Ac7003bce3f5C3585383F09486E2. mETH & stETH are currently being exchanged for ETH on DEX.

data.title: KAITO hits 2.12 USDT, setting a new record high
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: OKX market data shows that KAITO broke through 2.1 USDT, reaching a maximum of 2.12 USDT, setting a new historical high, and is currently trading at 2.04 USDT.

data.title: Apple Cancels Cloud Encryption Feature Following UK Order
data.description: According to Foresight News, Apple (AAPL.O) has decided to cancel its cloud encryption feature in response to a backdoor order issued by the UK government. Previously, Foresight News reported that Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin commented on the situation, stating that if Apple chooses to exit the UK market rather than compromise user data, he would purchase additional Apple devices.
